title: alnus glutinosa var. vulgaris
intro: Alnus glutinosa var. vulgaris, commonly known as the common alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the birch family. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa var. vulgaris include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
